page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at 2.8 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

A key architectural characteristic of the D48s_v5 is the complete absence of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware. While it lacks local NVMe or SSD drives, it is designated as Premium Storage capable, meaning persistent storage must be attached externally. The lack of integrated GPUs limits its utility for heavy parallel computing tasks like deep learning training, though it supports CPU-based instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512, and cryptography acceleration flags like AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks position the D48s_v5 as a highly capable system for multi-threaded workloads. It achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48423.66 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15438.0. The system demonstrates strong performance in specific areas, such as Passmark database operations at 21546.46 and CPU encryption tests at 42583.39, both ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory performance is also notable, with a Passmark memory score of 2811.61 and a cached read bandwidth of 2503.54 GB/sec. For lightweight AI workloads, it achieves a strong text generation speed of 285.41 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v5 offers a dense allocation of dedicated compute resources and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU accelerators. This makes it a resource-efficient option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage. The instance is well-suited for relational databases, enterprise applications, web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose business-critical workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 instance utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in database operations, CPU encryption, and memory throughput, with a Passmark CPU score of 48423.66 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15438.0. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in image processing and small-model LLM text generation. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D48s_v5 is highly suited for enterprise applications, database hosting, software compilation, and web serving workloads that rely on external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage. It delivers strong database and encryption performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for databases and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced, high-density compute and memory platform optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and secure multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192.0 GB RAM
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Strong benchmark performance in database operations and encryption
Best suited for relational databases, web serving, and enterprise applications
